title: 1、Project Overview
prompt: Provide a comprehensive project overview for the maas-portal-vue application. Explain that this is an enterprise-grade Model-as-a-Service (MaaS) management portal built with Vue 3 + TypeScript, serving as a frontend SPA for enterprise users to browse service packages, manage orders, monitor services, handle billing, and administer enterprise accounts. Detail the core value proposition (one-stop AI API service platform), the target user groups (enterprise administrators, developers, procurement teams), the project's current stage (v0.1.0 frontend prototype with demo authentication), and summarize all 11 business functional modules: Dashboard, Packages, Models, Orders, Services, Wallet, Billing, Enterprise, Accounts, and Audit. Reference the package.json name and version, and the README.md project description.

sections.title: project-positioning
sections.name: Project Positioning & Value Proposition
sections.prompt: Analyze the project's business positioning by examining README.md, package.json, and HomeView.vue. Explain how the portal positions itself as a 'one-stop enterprise MaaS API service platform' (智信MaaS服务管理平台). Detail the core value proposition: secure authentication, flexible package procurement, full lifecycle service management, transparent billing, and comprehensive audit trails. Reference the Chinese branding, the target enterprise market, and the competitive advantages highlighted in the landing page feature sections.

sections.title: tech-stack
sections.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
sections.prompt: Provide a detailed breakdown of the complete technology stack by analyzing package.json, vite.config.ts, and tsconfig.json. Cover: Vue 3.5 with Composition API and <script setup>, TypeScript 5.7, Vite 6.3 as build tool, Vue Router 4.5 with HTML5 History mode, Pinia 3.0 for state management, Tailwind CSS 4.1 with @tailwindcss/vite plugin, Radix Vue 1.9 as UI primitive foundation, Lucide Vue Next 0.564 for icons, and class-variance-authority 0.7 with clsx and tailwind-merge for component variant management. Explain the role and integration of each technology.

sections.title: core-modules
sections.name: Core Functional Modules
sections.prompt: Systematically document all 11 business functional modules identified in the codebase. For each module, list its route path, view component, content component, and core purpose. Modules are: Dashboard (/dashboard), Packages (/packages), Models (/models), Orders (/orders), Services (/services), Wallet (/wallet), Billing (/billing), Audit (/audit), Enterprise (/enterprise), Accounts (/accounts), and the Login/Landing page (/). Reference src/router/index.ts for route definitions and src/components/portal/Sidebar.vue for the navigation grouping structure (服务, 费用, 管理).

sections.title: project-structure
sections.name: Project Directory Structure
sections.prompt: Document the complete file organization and directory structure. Explain the purpose of each directory: public/ for static assets, src/main.ts as the application bootstrap, src/App.vue as the root router-view container, src/router/ for routing configuration, src/stores/ for Pinia state management, src/views/ for page-level view components, src/components/portal/ for business layout and content components, src/components/ui/ for the reusable UI component library, and src/lib/ for utility functions. Reference the actual file tree and explain the view-to-content component pattern used throughout the application.

title: 2、Environment Setup & Development Guide
prompt: Create a practical guide for setting up the development environment and working with the maas-portal-vue project. Cover system prerequisites, dependency installation, available npm scripts, development server configuration, build process, and deployment options. Reference package.json scripts, vite.config.ts server port configuration (3000), README.md quick start section, and the deployment examples (Nginx, Docker, Vercel/Netlify). Include the demo login credentials and explain session behavior.

sections.title: prerequisites
sections.name: System Prerequisites
sections.prompt: Document the system requirements for developing this project. Based on README.md and package.json, specify Node.js >= 18.0.0 and npm >= 9.0.0 as minimum requirements. Explain the ES module setup (type: module in package.json) and any global tooling expectations.

sections.title: installation
sections.name: Installation & Dependency Management
sections.prompt: Provide step-by-step installation instructions. Reference the README.md installation section: running npm install to install dependencies including Vue, Vite, Pinia, Vue Router, Tailwind CSS, Radix Vue, Lucide icons, and development tools (vue-tsc, @vitejs/plugin-vue, @tailwindcss/vite). Explain the purpose of key dependencies and devDependencies.

sections.title: development-workflow
sections.name: Development Workflow & Scripts
sections.prompt: Document all available npm scripts from package.json: dev (vite - development server on port 3000), build (vue-tsc -b && vite build - type checking followed by production build), and preview (vite preview - preview production build). Explain the build pipeline, how Vite processes Vue SFCs and Tailwind CSS, and the role of vue-tsc in type checking. Include the demo account credentials (admin@sample.com / admin123) for testing authentication.

sections.title: configuration-overview
sections.name: Project Configuration Overview
sections.prompt: Explain the key configuration files: vite.config.ts (Vue plugin, Tailwind plugin, @/ alias to ./src, server port 3000), tsconfig.json (TypeScript compiler options and path mapping), and index.html (HTML entry point). Reference the actual configuration values and explain how the @/ path alias is used throughout the codebase for imports.

sections.title: deployment
sections.name: Build & Deployment Options
sections.prompt: Document the deployment strategies described in README.md. Cover: static file hosting with Nginx (SPA fallback configuration), Docker multi-stage build (node:20-alpine builder + nginx:alpine runtime), and Vercel/Netlify deployment with redirect rules. Explain the dist/ output directory, asset caching strategies, and the importance of SPA route fallback for HTML5 History mode.

title: 3、Application Architecture
prompt: Provide a comprehensive architectural analysis of the maas-portal-vue application. Analyze the system design patterns, component hierarchy, data flow, and file organization. Reference src/main.ts (bootstrap sequence), src/App.vue (root container), src/router/index.ts (routing architecture), src/components/portal/PortalLayout.vue (layout system), and the view-to-content component pattern. Explain how the application initializes Pinia before Vue Router, restores authentication sessions, and mounts the app. Detail the lazy-loaded route components and the auth guard mechanism.

sections.title: system-design
sections.name: System Design & Architectural Patterns
sections.prompt: Analyze the overall architectural pattern of the application. Explain that it follows a classic Single Page Application (SPA) layered architecture with: a presentation layer (Vue components), a state management layer (Pinia stores), a routing layer (Vue Router), and a utility layer (lib/utils.ts). Detail the bootstrap sequence in src/main.ts: createApp -> createPinia -> use(pinia) -> restoreSession -> use(router) -> mount. Explain why Pinia must be initialized before restoring the session or using the router.

sections.title: component-hierarchy
sections.name: Component Hierarchy & Relationships
sections.prompt: Map the complete component hierarchy tree. Start from App.vue (<router-view/>), through route-level views (DashboardView.vue, etc.), to PortalLayout.vue (Sidebar + Header + main slot), down to business Content components (DashboardContent.vue, PackagesContent.vue, etc.), and finally to UI primitive components (Button.vue, Card.vue, Dialog.vue, etc. from src/components/ui/). Explain the layout wrapping pattern where all authenticated views use PortalLayout, while the landing page (HomeView.vue) is standalone.

sections.title: data-flow
sections.name: Data Flow & State Communication
sections.prompt: Analyze how data flows through the application. Explain the unidirectional data flow from Pinia stores (auth.ts) to components via composables (useAuthStore), prop passing from views to content components, event emission for user interactions, and router navigation for page transitions. Detail the auth state flow: login action -> sessionStorage persistence -> route guard evaluation -> conditional rendering in PortalLayout.vue. Explain the mock data patterns used in content components (stats arrays, package definitions, activity lists).

sections.title: file-organization
sections.name: File Organization & Module Boundaries
sections.prompt: Document the file organization principles and module boundaries. Explain the separation between views/ (thin page wrappers), components/portal/ (business logic and layout), components/ui/ (reusable design system primitives), stores/ (state management), router/ (routing logic), and lib/ (shared utilities). Analyze how this structure enforces separation of concerns and enables parallel development of business features and UI components.

title: 4、Authentication & Authorization System
prompt: Provide an in-depth technical analysis of the authentication and authorization system. Analyze src/stores/auth.ts (Pinia store with User/RegisterData interfaces, demo users, session persistence), src/router/index.ts (navigation guards with meta.requiresAuth), src/components/portal/PortalLayout.vue (auth state watcher), and src/views/HomeView.vue (login/register dialogs with form validation). Explain the complete authentication flow from user input through validation, demo credential matching, session storage, route protection, and logout.

sections.title: auth-store
sections.name: Authentication Store (Pinia)
sections.prompt: Deep dive into src/stores/auth.ts. Explain the store implementation using Pinia's Composition API style (defineStore with ref/computed). Document the User interface (name, company, role, email) and RegisterData interface (company, name, email, phone, password). Analyze the DEMO_USERS array with hardcoded demo credentials, the STORAGE_KEY constant for sessionStorage, and all store methods: restoreSession (reads from sessionStorage), login (async with 800ms delay, credential matching), register (async with 1000ms delay, email uniqueness check), logout (clears state and storage), clearJustLoggedIn, and the isLoggedIn computed property.

sections.title: login-flow
sections.name: Login Flow & Form Validation
sections.prompt: Analyze the login implementation in src/views/HomeView.vue. Document the login dialog state management (showLogin ref), form fields (email, password, showPassword, rememberMe), validation logic (required field checks), error handling (loginError display), loading states (loginSubmitting with Loader2 spinner), and the handleLogin async function that calls auth.login() and redirects to /dashboard on success. Explain the watch on auth.isLoggedIn that auto-redirects authenticated users away from the login page.

sections.title: register-flow
sections.name: Registration Flow & Validation Rules
sections.prompt: Analyze the registration implementation in src/views/HomeView.vue. Document the register dialog state (showRegister), form fields (company, name, regEmail, phone, regPassword, confirmPassword, agreed), the validateRegister function with comprehensive validation rules (required fields, email regex format, Chinese phone number regex /^1[3-9]\d{9}$/, password minimum 6 characters, password matching, terms agreement), error display per field, and the handleRegister async flow.

sections.title: route-guards
sections.name: Route Guards & Access Control
sections.prompt: Deep dive into src/router/index.ts navigation guards. Explain the router.beforeEach implementation that checks to.meta.requiresAuth against auth.isLoggedIn. Document the three guard branches: (1) redirecting unauthenticated users to Login when accessing protected routes, (2) redirecting authenticated users to Dashboard when accessing Login, and (3) allowing all other navigation. Analyze how the route meta fields ({ requiresAuth: true/false }) define access levels for all 11 routes.

sections.title: session-management
sections.name: Session Management & Persistence
sections.prompt: Analyze the session persistence mechanism. Explain how auth.restoreSession() in src/main.ts runs before router initialization to prevent flash-of-unauthenticated-content. Document the sessionStorage-based persistence (JSON serialization/deserialization), the isLoading state that prevents premature route evaluation, and the PortalLayout.vue watch on [auth.isLoading, auth.isLoggedIn] that redirects to / when the session expires or user logs out. Explain why sessionStorage (not localStorage) was chosen and its security implications.

title: 5、Portal Layout & Navigation System
prompt: Provide comprehensive documentation of the portal layout and navigation system. Analyze src/components/portal/PortalLayout.vue (the master layout with auth-based conditional rendering), src/components/portal/Sidebar.vue (collapsible navigation with grouped menu items), and src/components/portal/Header.vue (top bar with user menu, notifications, and logout). Explain how these three components form the consistent authenticated user experience across all 10 business modules.

sections.title: portal-layout
sections.name: Portal Layout Component
sections.prompt: Deep dive into src/components/portal/PortalLayout.vue. Explain its role as the master layout wrapper for all authenticated pages. Document the auth state watcher that redirects to /login when the user is not authenticated, the loading state display (Loader2 spinner), the three-zone layout structure (Sidebar + Header + main content slot), and how it provides consistent layout while allowing variable content through the <slot>. Analyze the CSS classes used for full-height flex layout and overflow handling.

sections.title: sidebar-navigation
sections.name: Sidebar Navigation
sections.prompt: Analyze src/components/portal/Sidebar.vue in detail. Document the menuGroups array structure with four groups (no label for Dashboard, 服务 for Packages/Models/Orders/Services, 费用 for Wallet/Billing, 管理 for Enterprise/Accounts/Audit). Explain the collapsible sidebar implementation (collapsed ref with w-16/w-60 transition), active route highlighting using route.path comparison, the logo and branding section (智信MaaS服务管理平台), the search input placeholder, and the Lucide icon integration for each menu item. Analyze the transition-all duration-300 CSS animation.

sections.title: header-system
sections.name: Header & User Menu System
sections.prompt: Analyze src/components/portal/Header.vue. Document the three main header elements: notification bell with badge (showing count of 3), help button, and user dropdown menu. Explain the user dropdown implementation using DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, and DropdownMenuItem components from the UI library. Detail the avatar display with initials extraction (auth.user?.name?.charAt(0)), company and role display, and the logout handler that calls auth.logout() and redirects to /. Reference the Badge component for the notification counter.

sections.title: route-structure
sections.name: Route Structure & Lazy Loading
sections.prompt: Document the complete routing architecture from src/router/index.ts. List all 11 RouteRecordRaw definitions with their paths, names, lazy-loaded component imports (() => import('@/views/...')), and meta.requiresAuth flags. Explain the HTML5 History mode configuration (createWebHistory) and the performance benefit of lazy loading each view component. Analyze the route naming convention and how route names are used in navigation guards and programmatic navigation.

title: 6、Dashboard Module
prompt: Provide comprehensive documentation of the Dashboard module. Analyze src/views/DashboardView.vue (thin wrapper) and src/components/portal/DashboardContent.vue (the actual dashboard implementation). Document the welcome section with personalized greeting, the four stats cards (已开通服务, 本月API调用, 待支付订单, 即将到期套餐), quick action shortcuts to common pages, the recent activities timeline, the active packages display with usage progress and status badges, and the enterprise verification dialog that appears on first login.

sections.title: dashboard-overview
sections.name: Dashboard Overview & Welcome Section
sections.prompt: Analyze the DashboardContent.vue top section. Document the personalized welcome message with hardcoded company name (中科云数科技), the 已认证企业 badge using the Badge component with ShieldCheck icon, and the overall page structure using the space-y-6 Tailwind utility for vertical spacing.

sections.title: stats-cards
sections.name: Statistics Cards Grid
sections.prompt: Deep dive into the stats cards implementation. Document the stats array with four metrics: 已开通服务 (5个, +2本月新增), 本月API调用 (128,456次, +12.5%), 待支付订单 (2笔, ¥3,600), 即将到期套餐 (1个, 15天后). Explain the responsive grid layout (grid-cols-4 on large screens), the Card/CardHeader/CardTitle/CardContent component composition, the TrendingUp icon for change indicators, and the visual hierarchy using text size and color utilities.

sections.title: quick-actions
sections.name: Quick Actions Panel
sections.prompt: Analyze the quickActions array and its rendering. Document the three shortcuts: 订购套餐 (/packages), 查看API密钥 (/services), 查看账单 (/billing). Explain the interactive button design with hover states (hover:bg-accent), icon containers with rounded-lg bg-muted backgrounds, and the ArrowRight indicator. Analyze how these shortcuts improve user workflow efficiency.

sections.title: recent-activities
sections.name: Recent Activities Timeline
sections.prompt: Document the recentActivities array with five mock activities covering service activation, payment success, auth completion, order creation, and admin login. Explain the status-based color coding (green for success, yellow for pending, blue for info) using conditional CSS classes on small dots, and the time display format. Analyze the lg:col-span-2 responsive layout that gives the activities panel more width than quick actions.

sections.title: enterprise-verification
sections.name: Enterprise Verification Dialog
sections.prompt: Analyze the enterprise verification dialog triggered by auth.justLoggedIn. Document the Dialog component usage, the three-step verification flow (企业信息管理, 上传资质材料, 等待审核), the four benefits grid (更高API调用额度, 专属客户经理, 企业级SLA保障, 优先技术支持), and the DialogFooter with two actions (我知道了 dismiss, 验证企业资质 navigation to /enterprise). Explain the onMounted lifecycle hook that checks justLoggedIn and clears the flag after showing.

title: 7、Service Ecosystem Modules
prompt: Provide comprehensive documentation of the four core service ecosystem modules: Packages, Models, Orders, and Services. Analyze src/components/portal/PackagesContent.vue (tiered pricing cards with purchase flow), src/components/portal/ModelsContent.vue, src/components/portal/OrdersContent.vue, and src/components/portal/ServicesContent.vue. Explain how these modules form the complete service procurement and management lifecycle within the MaaS platform.

sections.title: packages-system
sections.name: Packages & Subscription System
sections.prompt: Deep dive into src/components/portal/PackagesContent.vue. Document the packages array with three tiers (基础版 ¥1,999/月, 高级版 ¥5,999/月, 尊享版 ¥19,999/月), each with feature lists and popularity flags. Analyze the tierConfig object that maps tier IDs to icon components (Sparkles, Rocket, Crown) and gradient color schemes. Explain the responsive card grid, the '最受欢迎' badge on the advanced tier, the price display formatting with toLocaleString(), the feature checklist with Check icons in green circles, the hover animations (hover:-translate-y-1 hover:shadow-xl), and the purchase confirmation Dialog flow.

sections.title: models-marketplace
sections.name: Models Marketplace
Billing Module
sections.prompt: Deep dive into the Billing module. Analyze BillingContent.vue component structure. Explain the billing record display: invoice/bill listing with Table components, payment status tracking with Badge variants, amount and date formatting. Detail any filtering by date range, status, or amount. Cover the relationship between billing records, orders, and wallet transactions. Discuss billing cycle visualization and payment history patterns.

sections.title: audit-module
sections.name: Audit Module
sections.prompt: Deep dive into the Audit module. Analyze AuditContent.vue component structure. Explain the audit log display: chronological activity records with Table components, timestamps, user identification, action types, and resource details. Detail any filtering by date, user, action type, or resource. Cover the audit trail completeness and log entry structure. Discuss the compliance and security monitoring aspects of the audit system.

sections.title: enterprise-module
sections.name: Enterprise Module
sections.prompt: Deep dive into the Enterprise module. Analyze EnterpriseContent.vue component structure. Explain the enterprise information display and management: company profile, contact details, business configuration. Detail any form components for editing enterprise data (Input, Label, Select, Button). Cover the relationship between enterprise data and the auth store's User.company field. Discuss the role of enterprise information in the MaaS portal context.

sections.title: accounts-module
sections.name: Accounts Module
sections.prompt: Deep dive into the Accounts module. Analyze AccountsContent.vue component structure. Explain the sub-account/user management interface: user listing table, role display, status indicators. Detail account creation, editing, and deletion workflows using Dialog components for forms. Cover permission or role management using Select or Badge components. Discuss the relationship between accounts and the currently logged-in user's role ('超级管理员'). Analyze any invitation, activation, or suspension patterns.

title: 9、UI Component Library
prompt: Create a comprehensive analysis of the UI component library, which is a major architectural asset of the MaaS Portal Vue application. The library consists of 40 components under src/components/ui/, built as wrappers around Radix Vue headless primitives with Tailwind CSS styling. Explain the architectural philosophy: using Radix Vue for accessibility and behavior, while applying custom Tailwind CSS styling for the enterprise design system. Detail the component categorization: Layout (Card family), Form (Input, Select, Switch, Label), Data Display (Table family, Badge, Progress), Navigation (Tabs family, DropdownMenu family), Overlay (Dialog family), and Utility (Button, Separator, Avatar family). Analyze the class-variance-authority (CVA) pattern used for component variant management, enabling consistent style variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link for buttons; default, secondary, destructive, outline for badges). Explain the cn() utility function in src/lib/utils.ts that combines clsx and tailwind-merge for conditional class merging. Detail how components accept class props and merge them with base styles. Examine specific component implementations: Button with variant and size options, Card with its sub-components (CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter), Dialog with its composable sub-components, Table with its structural sub-components. Discuss the benefits of this component library approach: consistency, reusability, maintainability, and accessibility. Cover the integration with Tailwind CSS v4 and the tw-animate-css package for animations.

sections.title: component-architecture
sections.name: Component Architecture & Philosophy
sections.prompt: Analyze the UI component library architecture. Explain the design philosophy of wrapping Radix Vue primitives with Tailwind CSS styling. Detail the benefits: accessibility out-of-the-box from Radix Vue, consistent enterprise styling, composable component APIs. Explain the pattern of creating component families (e.g., Card with CardHeader, CardTitle, etc.) for complex UI patterns. Discuss the relationship between the component library and the shadcn-vue design system pattern.

sections.title: styling-system
sections.name: Styling System & Variant Management
sections.prompt: Deep dive into the styling system. Explain the cn() utility function in src/lib/utils.ts combining clsx (conditional classes) and tailwind-merge (Tailwind class conflict resolution). Analyze the class-variance-authority (CVA) usage in components like Button and Badge for variant management. Detail the variant definitions: button variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) and sizes (default, sm, lg, icon); badge variants (default, secondary, destructive, outline). Explain how components merge user-provided class props with base and variant styles.

sections.title: layout-components
sections.name: Layout Components
sections.prompt: Analyze the Card component family in detail. Explain Card.vue, CardHeader.vue, CardTitle.vue, CardDescription.vue, CardContent.vue, and CardFooter.vue. Detail how these composable pieces work together to create flexible card layouts. Analyze the Separator.vue component for visual division. Discuss the Tabs family (Tabs.vue, TabsList.vue, TabsTrigger.vue, TabsContent.vue) for content organization. Explain the styling patterns and Tailwind CSS classes used.

sections.title: form-components
sections.name: Form Components
sections.prompt: Analyze the form input components. Detail Input.vue (text input styling), Select family (Select.vue, SelectTrigger.vue, SelectContent.vue, SelectItem.vue) for dropdown selection, Switch.vue for toggle inputs, and Label.vue for form labeling. Explain the Radix Vue primitives that power each component and the custom Tailwind styling applied. Discuss form accessibility patterns and focus states.

sections.title: data-display-components
sections.name: Data Display Components
sections.prompt: Analyze the data display components. Detail the Table family (Table.vue, TableHeader.vue, TableBody.vue, TableHead.vue, TableRow.vue, TableCell.vue) for tabular data presentation. Explain Badge.vue for status indicators with variant support. Analyze Progress.vue for progress visualization. Discuss the styling consistency across these components and their usage patterns in the portal modules.

sections.title: overlay-navigation-components
sections.name: Overlay & Navigation Components
sections.prompt: Analyze the overlay and navigation components. Detail the Dialog family (Dialog.vue, DialogContent.vue, DialogHeader.vue, DialogTitle.vue, DialogDescription.vue, DialogFooter.vue) for modal interactions. Explain the DropdownMenu family (DropdownMenu.vue, DropdownMenuTrigger.vue, DropdownMenuContent.vue, DropdownMenuItem.vue, DropdownMenuSeparator.vue) for context menus. Discuss the Avatar family (Avatar.vue, AvatarFallback.vue) for user profile display. Explain the Button component with its comprehensive variant and size system. Cover the integration of Lucide Vue Next icons within these components.

title: 10、Utilities & Development Guide
prompt: Provide a comprehensive guide to the utility layer and development practices in the MaaS Portal Vue application. Focus on src/lib/utils.ts and its cn() function that forms the foundation of the component styling system. Explain how clsx provides conditional class construction and tailwind-merge resolves Tailwind CSS class conflicts, enabling flexible component styling. Analyze the global style.css file and Tailwind CSS v4 configuration. Detail the development workflow: running dev server, building for production, type checking with vue-tsc. Explain the TypeScript configuration (tsconfig.json) and its compiler options for Vue 3 development. Cover the env.d.ts file for Vite client type declarations. Discuss the public directory assets (icons, logos, placeholders) and their usage. Provide guidance on extending the application: adding new routes, creating new modules following the established patterns, adding new UI components, and integrating with a real backend API. Cover best practices for the Composition API with <script setup>, Pinia store patterns, and component design following the established conventions.

sections.title: utility-functions
sections.name: Utility Functions
sections.prompt: Analyze the utility layer. Deep dive into src/lib/utils.ts and the cn() function: explain how it combines clsx for conditional class arrays and tailwind-merge for deduplicating/conflict-resolving Tailwind classes. Provide usage examples showing how components call cn(baseClasses, variantClasses, props.class). Discuss the type safety through ClassValue type import. Explain why this utility is essential for the component variant system.

sections.title: styling-configuration
sections.name: Styling & Tailwind Configuration
sections.prompt: Analyze the styling system configuration. Explain the global style.css file and how Tailwind CSS v4 is integrated via the @tailwindcss/vite plugin. Detail the Tailwind CSS v4 features used: utility-first approach, custom color palettes if configured, animation utilities from tw-animate-css. Discuss the migration from Tailwind v3 to v4 patterns if applicable. Explain how the component library leverages Tailwind's utility classes consistently.

sections.title: typescript-configuration
sections.name: TypeScript Configuration
sections.prompt: Analyze the TypeScript setup. Explain tsconfig.json compiler options optimized for Vue 3 development. Detail the env.d.ts file providing Vite client type declarations. Discuss the type safety patterns used throughout the project: interfaces for User and RegisterData, prop typing in components, Pinia store typing. Cover the vue-tsc integration for build-time type checking.

sections.title: development-workflow
sections.name: Development Workflow
sections.prompt: Detail the development workflow and commands. Explain npm run dev (Vite dev server with HMR), npm run build (vue-tsc type checking + Vite production build), npm run preview (preview production build locally). Discuss the development experience: hot module replacement, Vue DevTools integration, TypeScript IntelliSense. Cover debugging strategies for Vue 3 Composition API and Pinia stores.

sections.title: extension-guide
sections.name: Application Extension Guide
sections.prompt: Provide guidance on extending the MaaS Portal Vue application. Explain how to add new routes following the existing pattern in src/router/index.ts (path, name, component lazy import, meta.requiresAuth). Detail the module creation pattern: create a View in src/views/, create a Content component in src/components/portal/, add sidebar navigation in Sidebar.vue. Explain how to add new UI components following the Radix Vue + Tailwind + CVA pattern. Discuss backend API integration strategies: replacing demo auth with real API, implementing API client modules, handling loading and error states. Cover state management expansion for new business domains.
